title: Fix MongoDB Query to Handle Missing Fields in Projection

statement.markdown: You are given a MongoDB query that uses the `$project` aggregation stage to select specific fields from a collection. However, the query fails when the field specified in the projection is missing from the document. Your task is to fix the query to handle missing fields correctly.
statement.inputFormat: A JSON object representing the MongoDB collection
statement.outputFormat: The modified query that handles missing fields
statement.notes: The query should only return the specified fields from the document, even if they are missing.

constraints.description: Number of documents in the collection

examples.explanation: The query should return only the specified fields, even though "city" is missing
The query should return only the specified fields, even though "age" is null

hints.text: Use the `$ifNull` aggregation operator to handle missing fields

editorial.title: Editorial: Handling Missing Fields in MongoDB Query
editorial.intuition: The `$project` stage should use the `$ifNull` operator to handle missing fields
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force solution: use the `$ifNull` operator to handle missing fields
editorial.betterSolution: Use the `$ifNull` operator with the `$project` stage
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution: use the `$ifNull` operator with the `$project` stage to handle missing fields
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The `$ifNull` operator returns the specified value if the field is missing
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: handle missing fields with `$ifNull`
